clauses_raw.clause_text_raw: Nothing in this bylaw shall prevent the use of land in any zone for:
Public streets;
Open space
Landscaped area;
Passive recreation;
Parks;
Utility services; and
Parking lots that meet the zone requirements.

No truck, bus, coach, recreational vehicle or trailer or streetcar body, nor a structure of any kind other than a dwelling unit erected and used in accordance with this and all other municipal bylaws shall be used for human habitation.
No vehicle body, truck trailer, or container shall be used as a commercial or accessory building except as specifically permitted by other legislation.
Temporary plastic garages are prohibited in all zones.
A boat, motor home or piece of heavy equipment such as construction equipment or a commercial truck shall not to be stored in the front yard of a residential property for more than six (6) months.

Where two (2) or more permitted uses are proposed in one (1) building or on one (1) lot, and when the regulations applicable to these uses are different, the most restrictive regulations shall be deemed to be in force for that lot or building, unless otherwise specified.
Existing uses are considered permitted.

Subject to the provisions of this bylaw, a specific use of land or a building, which is lawfully in existence on the effective date of this bylaw, and which does not conform to the permissible use of the zone where it is located, may continue to exist.
Any change of occupants or tenants of any premises or building shall not of itself be deemed to affect the use of the premises or building for the purposes of this bylaw.

A non-conforming use of land or building shall not be permitted to resume if it has been discontinued for a period of six (6) consecutive months, and in such event the land or building shall not thereafter be used except in conformity with this bylaw.

Where a non-conforming commercial use is occupying a building and the owner is requesting to convert the building or part thereof to residential use, and it is determined the number of units and use will not adversely affect the neighbourhood by way of traffic to the site, the Development Officer may, after receiving the plans, allow the building to be used for residential purposes.
Attached Accessory Dwelling Units (ADU) are permitted in RN, RM, DN, and DMU Zones only.
One (1) attached ADU is permitted in a single-detached dwelling, semi-detached or duplex subject to the following conditions:
The lot size is no less than 500 m2;
The zone requirements and building code requirements must be met;
The attached ADU has no more than two (2) bedrooms;
One (1) additional parking space shall be provided on the same lot;
The main dwelling unit on the subject property is a principal residence unit for the owner; and
The unit can be used as a short-term rental as long as the operator/host is present in the principal dwelling unit during the time of stay.
Two (2) attached ADU units are permitted in a single-detached dwelling subject to the following conditions:
The lot size is no less than 1,130 m2;
The zone requirements and building Code requirements must be met;
Each attached ADU has no more than two (2) bedrooms;
Two (2) additional parking spaces shall be provided on the same lot, unless the unit is within 400 m of a bus stop, in which case, only one (1) is required;
The additional parking space(s) are not located in the front yard;
The main dwelling unit on the subject property is a principal residence unit for the owner;
One of the units, but not both, can be used as a short-term rental as long as the operator/host is present in the principal dwelling unit during the time of stay.
No additional secondary suites (as defined in the National Building Code) shall be permitted.
The addition of any external staircase taller than 2 m shall address the following design controls:
External staircases shall not be located on any facade facing a public street.
External staircases shall be constructed of materials that are visually compatible with the principal dwelling and shall be designed as a subordinate architectural element.
The staircase shall not project into any yard setbacks.

Exterior entry ways for accessory dwelling units shall face side or rear yards. No more than two entry ways can face the street frontage. Example 1 - One Attached ADU • Min. lot area - 500 m 2 • One unit is the principal residence unit of the owner • Meets zone requirements for coverage and setbacks • Can be used as short-term rental • One additional parking space provided Example 2 - One Attached ADU • Min. lot area - 500 m 2 • One unit is the principal residence unit of the owner • Meets zone requirements for coverage and setbacks • Can be used as short-term rental • One additional parking space provided Example 3 - Two Attached ADUs • Min. lot area - 1130 m 2 • One unit is the principal residence unit of the owner • Meets zone requirements for coverage and setbacks • One unit can be used as short- term rental • Two additional parking spaces provided Figure 4.2 2-unit Attached ADU Example Figure 4.3 3-unit Attached ADU Example Figure 4.1 2-unit Attached ADU Example
Detached Accessory Dwelling Units (ADU) are permitted in RN, RM, DN, and DMU Zones only.
One (1) detached ADU may be permitted on a single detached lot, semi lot, duplex lot, or townhouse lot if the subject property meets the following requirements:
Has a minimum lot area of 500 m2;
The combined footprint of the ADU and principal dwelling does not exceed the lot coverage requirements of the zone plus 10%;
The ADU is located in the side or rear yard and not the front or flankage yard;
Provides one (1) additional parking space on the same lot unless the lot is within 800 m of a transit stop;
The additional parking space is not located in the front yard;
The ADU is connected to municipal services.
The detached ADU shall be subject to the following design requirements:
Meets the requirements in Table 4.1
Basements or any below grade construction is not permitted except where the slopes permit a walk-out unit.
Can be used as a short-term rental under the condition that the operator/ host must be present in the principal dwelling unit during the time of stay.
No secondary suite (as defined in the National Building Code) shall be permitted as a detached structure.
Group Homes shall be permitted in the following zone(s) depending on the classification/type of group home:
Group Home Limited: Permitted in the RM Zone and only within a single- detached dwelling;
Group Home: Permitted in the RM, RH, DN, and DMU Zones and within a dwelling unit or an institutional facility.
The following regulations will apply to both categories of group home(s):
Maintain a minimum radius of 300 metres to another group home for those areas not in the 500 Lot Area;
Requires one (1) off street parking space per three (3) beds, plus one (1) off street parking space per two (2) employees on a shift;

Not permitted as a lodging house, Transitional housing facility, tourist accommodation or home occupation. • One additional parking space provided, not in rear yard. • One additional parking space provided, not in rear yard. • One additional parking space provided, not in rear yard.
Home Occupations may be permitted in any Neighbourhood or Downtown zones provided that:

The total Floor Area used for the home occupation shall not exceed 25% of the Gross Floor Area of the principal dwelling up to a maximum area of 46.5 m2 (500 ft2) within the dwelling; or, when the home occupation operates from an accessory building, the accessory building shall meet the regulations for maximum Gross Floor Area permitted on the property;
The proprietor(s) of the home occupation shall live in the primary dwelling in which the home occupation is located, and it shall be the proprietor(s) principal residence;

There shall be a maximum of one (1) full-time employee or two (2) part-time employees, other than the proprietor, working inside or outside of the dwelling. The proprietor(s) shall not exceed these requirements by contracting work out to other persons (employees) not located in the dwelling.
There shall be no external storage of materials and no outdoor animal enclosures which would indicate that any part of the dwelling or lot is being used for any purpose other than a residential use;
There are no other home occupations on the property or in the dwelling;
No more than one (1) commercial vehicle shall be parked or stored on the lot, but notwithstanding the foregoing, other vehicles may be parked or stored on the lot in a wholly enclosed building;
Parking shall be subject to the parking regulations of this bylaw;
Signage shall be subject to the signage regulations of this bylaw;
Required parking for home occupations may be provided in tandem with parking for the main residence;
Are limited to one per lot; and
Are not permitted within a multi-unit dwelling or an attached ADU.
If alterations or renovations are required for the operation of the home occupation the proprietor shall obtain a building permit before commencing operation of the home occupation.
The following uses are permitted as home occupations:
Home daycare, subject to the bylaw provisions for home daycares;
Broadcast and production studio use;
Home office, clerical, computer and/or telephone-based services;
Catering use;
Grooming and personal care salon;
Pet grooming;
Pet daycare up to five (5) animals;
Medical clinic use;
Custom sewing, crafts, or production of visual arts studio use (i.e. painting, pottery, wood carving);
Office use;
Additional uses, including uses with appointments typically on an hourly basis or as scheduled, may be considered following the process of review for a major variance;

Tailoring or shoecare; Four (4) bedrooms are permitted for the first 370 m2 (3,982.8 ft2) of lot area, and for every additional bedroom over four (4) the lot must be increased by 100 m2 (1076.4 ft2), up to a maximum of 7 bedrooms.
Tattooing;
Tutoring;
The following uses are prohibited as home occupations:
Automobile body shop;
Automotive sales beyond selling one personal vehicle;
Eating and drinking establishment;
Retail style operations;
Welding and metal fabrication; and
Industrial uses that cause strong smells or loud noises.
A home daycare is a permitted home occupation, subject to the following requirements:
It shall be located on a local street where on-street parking is permitted;
It shall comply with all provincial requirements of the Child Care Facilities Act R.S.P.E.I. 1988, Cap. C-5 and amendments thereto;
The maximum gross floor area for a home occupation does not apply for a home daycare;
Daycare services classified as a commercial daycare centre must be licensed under the Child Care Facilities Act R.S.P.E.I. 1988, Cap. C-5.
Tourist accommodations, such as a heritage inn, hostel, AirBnB/VRBO, or tourist home, are permitted, subject to the following requirements:
It is licensed under the Tourism Industry Act;
No kitchen and/or cooking facilities are permitted in a guest room;
Short term rental accessory to a residential use in Table 4.2 shall be permitted provided that the dwelling unit is the primary residence of the short-term rental operator;
A secondary or garden suite may be rented as a short term rental if the operator/host is present in the Principal Residence during the time of stay;
The operator/host does not have to be present at time of stay if the entire dwelling unit is rented;
No more than one (1) booking may be permitted for tourist accommodation rental lodging in each dwelling unit at one time;
Parking shall be subject to the parking regulations of this bylaw; and
Signage shall be subject to the signage regulations of this bylaw; and
Not permitted in apartment dwellings.

cells_raw.cell_text_raw: GC, GN, DC, DN, DMU, and DW Zones

Four (4) bedrooms are permitted for the first 370 m2 (3,982.8 ft2) of lot area, and for every additional bedroom over four (4) the lot must be increased by 100 m2 (1076.4 ft2), up to a maximum of 7 bedrooms.

clauses_raw.clause_text_raw: A Mobile Canteen may be permitted:
In any zone for which eating and drinking establishments are permitted;
On a publicly owned property within the PPS Zone, to support an active recreation land use, subject to approval by the City for a short term special event or events; and
On a street right-of-way, subject to the Street Vendors Bylaw.
Mobile canteens in the downtown shall be permitted to operate between May 1st and October 31st, at which time the mobile canteen must be removed from the property.

Mobile canteens outside of the downtown on private property shall be permitted to operate for a twelve (12) month period, at which time the mobile canteen must be removed or the applicant must reapply.
A mobile canteen shall not exceed 20 m2 (215.3 ft2).
The minimum Landscaped Area requirement shall not be diminished for the property in which the Mobile Canteen is located.

The mobile canteen shall not be required to provide additional parking spaces on the property; however, the minimum parking space standards shall not be diminished for the property in which the mobile canteen is located.
An application for a mobile canteen shall include the following information:
A site plan accurately drawn to scale that shows;
The location of the mobile canteen on the site and its proximity to other structures or buildings on the site;
The location of existing and proposed parking spaces; and
The location of garbage receptacles.
A photo of the mobile canteen from which merchandise will be sold;
Confirmation of compliance from the Charlottetown Fire Department where the mobile canteen is equipped with a cooking and/or heating apparatus;
Confirmation of compliance that the mobile canteen meets all provincial health regulations and inspections; and
Written authorization from the property owner granting permission to use the property for the purpose applied for and for time frame requested.
The Street Vending Permit as granted and issued may not be transferred, assigned, conveyed or sold to another applicant or person.
Nothing in this bylaw shall prevent a licensee from holding more than one license issued pursuant to this bylaw; however, only one (1) mobile canteen shall be permitted per property.
No structural modifications may be made to the mobile canteen or equipment placed herein without prior approval of the Development Officer and/or Fire Inspector.

Where any outdoor site in any zone is used for an outdoor salvage or waste disposal facility, auto-wrecking, recycling depot or scrapyard facility; or an outdoor facility for assembly, production, storage, repair or distribution of goods or products, the following conditions shall apply:

The whole of the operation (excluding the front yard) shall be surrounded by an opaque fence not less than 2.5 m (8 ft) and not greater than 3.7 m (12 ft) in height, and which is continuous except for gates necessary for access to the operation; and
Any land not required for entrance and exit driveways and which is located between the fence and any lot line shall only be used for a landscaped area.
Outside storage or stockpiling of goods shall not:
Cause a nuisance to adjoining properties by way of dust; and
Be stockpiled higher than the primary building on the lot and where the lot has no building, the stockpile shall be no higher than 6.1 m (20 ft).
Where any property is used for an outdoor industrial operation, including storage or display, such an operation shall be established behind the minimum setback for buildings in that zone.

sections_raw.section_title_raw: AUTOMOBILE SERVICE STATIONS AND GAS BARS

clauses_raw.clause_text_raw: Where a lot is used for an automobile service station, the following requirements shall apply:
The minimum lot frontage shall be 38 m (124.7 ft);
No more that 2 vehicular ingress/egress points are permitted per street frontage with driveway widths a maximum of 10 m wide;
The minimum lot depth shall be 30 m (98.4 ft);
The turning radii at the vehicular ingress/egress point shall be a minimum of 9 m;
A pump island shall setback at least 6.1 m (20 ft) from the street line;
a canopy or roof over a pump island shall meet the minimum setback required for the primary building, or 5 m (17 ft) from the street, whichever is less; and
A Development Agreement is required for all new gas bars.

No person may strip, excavate, or otherwise remove top soil for sale or use from a lot or other parcel of land or operate an excavation pit as defined in the excavation pit Regulations of the Environmental Protection Act, R.S.P.E.I. 1988, Cap. E-9.

Where a building permit has been issued or where a subdivision plan has been approved, and where there is an excess of soil material other than that required for grading and landscaped areas on the lot, such excess may be removed for use or sale.

The City may enter into an agreement for the removal of excess soil material or the infill of a lot or the dumping of material and place any requirements it deems necessary to protect adjacent land or the environment provided the work is done in accordance with the Environmental Protection Act, R.S.P.E.I. 1988, Cap. E-9, and its regulations, as amended from time to time.

Any change to existing elevations that will impact the existing surface water drainage patterns onto adjoining lots or onto the public street or Right-of-way shall require approval by the jurisdiction having authority.

sections_raw.section_title_raw: USE OF UNDEVELOPED LANDS IN ALL ZONES

clauses_raw.clause_text_raw: A parcel of undeveloped land in any residential zone that has at least 100 m (325 ft) of lot frontage and 1 hectare (2.45 acres) in lot area may be used, prior to its subdivision, servicing or development, for any of the following purposes:
Single-detached dwelling;
Farm, excluding intensive livestock use or production;
Agricultural field crop or pasture;
Market garden;
Greenhouse; and
Forestry related.

sections_raw.section_title_raw: RESIDENTIAL DEVELOPMENT NEAR A SEWAGE LAGOON OR TREATMENT PLANT

clauses_raw.clause_text_raw: Notwithstanding any other provision of this bylaw, no residential dwelling shall be located within 45 m (147.6 ft) of a sewage lagoon or treatment plant.
Cannabis retail sales uses and cannabis lounge uses are only permitted if such facilities are operated by the PEI Cannabis Management Corporation.
Notwithstanding any other provision of this bylaw, a cannabis retail store shall not be located within 300 m (984.2 ft) of the following land uses:
School;
Park, with play equipment intended for children;
Daycare or other early learning facility licensed under the Child Care Facilities Act R.S.P.E.I. 1988, Cap. C-5.
Notwithstanding any other provision of this bylaw a cannabis retail store shall not be located within 1,000 m (3,280.8 ft) of an existing cannabis retail store.

The provisions under this section are to be applied at the time of the establishment of a new cannabis retail store and are not intended to restrict any of the protected uses noted above from locating within the required separation distance after the cannabis retail store is established.
